creator: tsoi@holywar.net
text:
It works well. Thank you for your patch.


Thus spake Jacques A. Vidrine (n@nectar.com) :::::

: On Fri, Dec 29, 2000 at 05:21:30AM +0000, tsoi@holywar.net wrote:
: > kerberized telnetd does not consult /etc/login.conf, so can't
: > control any users by using /etc/login.conf
: 
: Actually, telnetd is not responsible for consulting login.conf.  That
: is the job of the login program.  MIT Kerberos actually provides its
: own login program (login.krb5), and that is what is used by telnetd.
: If you would like to use login in the base system, the following patch
: should do the trick (put this in ports/security/krb5/files):
:  
: --- appl/telnet/telnetd/Makefile.in.orig	Fri Dec 29 06:56:06 2000
: +++ appl/telnet/telnetd/Makefile.in	Fri Dec 29 06:56:19 2000
: @@ -24,7 +24,7 @@
:  #	@(#)Makefile.generic	5.5 (Berkeley) 3/1/91
:  #
:  
: -AUTH_DEF=-DAUTHENTICATION -DENCRYPTION -DKRB5 -DFORWARD -UNO_LOGIN_F -ULOGIN_CAP_F -DLOGIN_PROGRAM=KRB5_PATH_LOGIN
: +AUTH_DEF=-DAUTHENTICATION -DENCRYPTION -DKRB5 -DFORWARD -UNO_LOGIN_F -ULOGIN_CAP_F
:  OTHERDEFS=-DKLUDGELINEMODE -DDIAGNOSTICS -DENV_HACK -DOLD_ENVIRON
:  LOCALINCLUDES=-I.. -I$(srcdir)/..
:  DEFINES = $(AUTH_DEF) $(OTHERDEFS)
: 
: Please let me know how this works for you.
: 
: Cheers,
: -- 
: Jacques Vidrine / n@nectar.com / jvidrine@verio.net / nectar@FreeBSD.org

--
